title: $105,000 Faceless YouTube Niche, YT Algorithm Updates, Roblox & Brainrot | YT NEWS #2

transcript.text: The most important news of the week is that I invested in the fourth YouTube channel this year. This one in particular is very special. I cannot reveal the niche right now, but I'm very excited to see how far this channel can go. We truly have just one competitor in a niche and uh this guy is getting 13 million views on average. So, we will be the first ones to try and post 30 to 40 videos a month in that niche. And uh half a billion views is the potential that I'm looking to get on this particular channel. And of course, I'll update you weekly on how we scale it from this moment on. A portfolio of our YouTube shorts channels made $10,000 last week. It wasn't the best week I've seen. I remember hitting $20,000 weeks with just one of these channels. But in YouTube business, it's uh always ups and downs. and then apps again. Taking into account that one of these channels was falsely terminated and then reinstated uh last week, I'm pretty happy with a 5 figure result this week. And because we've just added a fourth channel to this portfolio, soon we'll see way more than 20 and 30k weeks. Now, as usual, this news digest will have a lot of sauce, but this one is very special. First, I'll show you the most important news of the week. And uh there are a few that are really gamechanging for uh creators, especially faceless creators. Then I'll show you the top performing formats that you can start using right now, no matter the niche. And then at the end, my team prepared a list of seven untapped niches that are blowing up this week. So you can simply copy one of them and get started with Faceless YouTube automation. So without further ado, let's get started. YouTube Shorts has finally launched this massive update on the official website. We are enabling Shorts creators to link the brand's websites to their branded content. This is absolutely huge because when I, a faceless creator with over 1.5 million followers and over four billion views, tried reaching out to brands to sponsor my content, I got laughed at. Nobody wanted to give me a single penny for a sponsored short. That is because you couldn't add a link directly to uh the video, meaning brands couldn't track the effectiveness of a campaign. And I was so jealous looking at the lawn form creators who are charging 5 to 10 grand for a 30 secondond sponsored integration in a video that would barely get more than 100k views while I was sitting on videos getting hundreds of millions of views. But this is finally going to change. Uh this is how the uh sponsored post is going to uh look like. And the channels like Art of War, for example, uh will finally be able to charge $3 to $5,000 for a simple YouTube shirt. Sora will now be able to AI generate videos based on characters from Disney, Marvel, Pixar, and Star Wars. So if you ever tried to uh generate Disney characters with charity, you know that it gives you absolutely nothing because Disney wanted to protect its IP. However, last week Disney invested a billion dollars into open AI and now Sora and I guess Charpt as well will be able to generate pictures and videos of our favorite characters. I can only imagine how many short-term opportunities it will open for faceless YouTube creators. Uh but uh be careful because uh this is still uh using someone else's IP to promote your stuff. Another massive YouTube news this week. YouTube has finally launched Ask Studio. This is essentially an AI inside your YouTube dashboard uh designed to consult you on your YouTube video performance. For example, you can ask why didn't my last video perform? Or for example, what video brought in the most subscribers? To me, it sounds useful as a navigation tool because uh YouTube analytics might be confusing. It looks like a bunch of uh buttons here and there like you're trying to launch a rocket, especially if you're a beginner. But uh now you can just ask AI whatever you want to know about your audience and uh it will simply guide you uh through it. And of course, I wanted to test it out. But uh when I opened my channel dashboard, I learned that uh this thing is only available in US, Canada, and India. So well, Indians are enjoying YouTube's new AI. We creators from Europe are going to do the analytics the old way. Fortune breaking news. YouTube launches option for US creators to receive stable coin payouts through PayPal. Now, when choosing a payment option in your AdSense, you can not only choose a bank account, but also a payout in stable coins through PayPal. Before, it was an extra benefit that MCN were providing you with, but now YouTube is doing the exact same thing. I bet more platforms will follow Tik Tok, Facebook, and others. And soon, every creator on Earth will withdraw money in crypto. Another YouTube update this week. You can now reply to your comments on YouTube with a voice note. Neil Moan, the CEO of YouTube, tweeted it saying, "YouTube teams continue to ship relentlessly for our creators." Well, Neil, instead of launching yet another useless update, maybe try fixing your [ __ ] AI dock, the one that's mindlessly bulldozing the very creators you ship relentlessly for. YouTube Create has officially launched in the US. Essentially, it's a free mobile editing app made by YouTube. Think of it like Cap Cut, but uh everything is free. I don't know who's going to use it, but now you know it exists. All right. Now, I'm going to give you a few viral formats that been blowing up on YouTube uh this week. So, no matter the niche, you can adapt it and somehow use it for uh your channel because that's the stuff that works on YouTube. Now, this stupid video compilation got 820k views and made this creator $3,000. And I'm not the one to discuss compilation niches. As you know, I'm into branded long-term channels. But what I find interesting is the format and the thumbnail of this video because this was literally the first long form video they posted on the channel after a couple of unsuccessful shorts and it exploded to almost a million views in 2 weeks. So, what's up with the format for this video? They used Roblox versus real life in a whiteboard sticker style format. And there are so many good niches where you can adopt the same exact style. It reminds me of the explained with a duck channel, but basically any semi-educational channel can now adopt this style as it's something that blows up on YouTube right now. Mr. Bis has changed the thumbnail for one versus 100K firework and the views quintipled. I had to learn a new word to say this, but uh there's a huge lesson in why he changed it. The after thumbnail is a typical comparison thumbnail like the one we discussed with the Roblox Brain Rod. When you split your screen into two or even three sections, you instantly give the viewer more context on what's going to happen next. And that's why it drives way more views. A very interesting experiment that I saw on Twitter this week. This guy, Jack Bryant, posted a video with two similar thumbnails, but different titles using a new AB test feature we discussed last week. And while most people would expect that titles don't really matter as long as the thumbnail is the same, one of them actually got 77% watchtime share, which is almost double of what the other one got. This goes to show that viewers decide whether to watch the video or not based on the combination of thumbnail and title, not just on one specific thing. And one more thing I wanted to give you in the format section before I show you the best faceless YouTube niches we found is a billion views triangle. You see, let me tell you a secret that no one in the faceless YouTube space will ever tell you about making content. Most people think a good hook is what makes a video go viral. Some say it's a good story, good analogy, example, etc. But when I think about all of the YouTube shorts and even long form videos that we posted in the past year, the ones that have performed the best, it wasn't because the delivery was strong. It wasn't because even the thumbnail or title was strong. It was because the topic was something that people cared about. The topic had what Patty Galloway calls the CCN fit, which is core casual and new audience fit, which for YouTube I'll change to CNN fit, regular casual new viewers fit. This means the topic resonates with your regular audience, people who have followed you for a long time, your casual audience, people who follow you occasionally, and your new audience, people who have never heard about you before. You can, by the way, check it on your channel. You go to reports, then uh you click on audience size and growth, and you choose new, casual, and regular as a filter. Then just make sure that your regular viewers graph is increasing months after months. And that's how you grow your overall audience. Now, finally, let's get to the most awaited part of this digest. I'm going to give you the seven faceless YouTube niches that are printing hundreds of thousands of dollars right now. And this week, we have some amazing channels going on here. A faceless YouTube channel generated $30,000 because of a German girl with a beer they put on their thumbnail. This channel has only posted 20 videos, got 6 million views, and most of its views came from two video outliers, both having the same German lady with a beer on their thumbnail. I don't know what's up with this ladies, but recently another video with a similar girl exploded on a completely separate channel generated 13x more views than usual. So, if you're running some kind of a 2D animated channel, my totally serious, highly scientific recommendation is Busty German Women with Beer. The next faceless YouTube channel generated [sighs] $15,000 with only 12 videos, meaning 100K of it is pure profit. They're creating 2D animated videos in the best niche of all, finance. And their most popular video is a breakdown of how the Wolf of Wall Street scam worked. But they still have two weaknesses that if you fix, you can dominate the niche right now. Number one is volume. Because they likely hire on Upwork and Fiverr, they're definitely overpaying for freelancers and it takes them 3 weeks to complete a single video. With 2D animators that we hire for our clients, a video like that would take us 3 to 5 days to uh complete. Meaning, if you can come into that niche with 3 to 5x the volume, you can quickly monopolize it for yourself. And number two is ideiation. Because the moment a video about Wolf of Wall Street exploded to 2.5 million views, they should have learned from what worked and then tried to scale it. But instead, they kept posting some random ideas that basically flopped after that. I would be recommending this amazing channel and niche to my clients because with our ideation and hiring systems, one of them could easily print 30 to 40k per months just like that. So, you can also steal it if you're there. The Rock in the Space posted eight videos, generated $44,000, averaging around $5,000 per upload. Their most popular videos got five and 3 million views, and their strategy is so simple, anyone can steal it. So, the reason why this channel is so popular is the high barrier to entry. The 2D animations they produce would cost thousands of dollars if you try to hire an animator on Upwork and Fiverr. But if you go on Telegram and find communities of 2D animators from these countries, you can actually cut those costs by 5x. I, by the way, did exactly that, hiring for all three of our branded 3D and 2D channels that we run right now. And my margin is still 63% in some of the most complicated niches on YouTube. And if you want to steal and adopt this format for yourself, also pay close attention to the three-way split thumbnails because I've seen those work across multiple channels. Overall, a very good niche to take inspiration from. Very untapped if you know how to hire. This YouTube Shorts channel posted 57 shorts, gained 1 million subscribers, and generated 1.2 billion views. All thanks to the powerful strategy nobody seems to notice. This channel is posting Roblox animations and their most popular video got 150 million views, meaning it generated 15,000 from a 30 secondond clip. I call it the content upgrade method. When you find something that is already getting billions of views, like Roblox in-game footage videos, for example, and you upgrade it quality with animation, which allows more creative freedom. With Roblox, you can use Roblox Studio and Blender to animate those unique videos yourself or find an investor and hire a team of animators right away to scale faster. This is what I did with one of my first channels when I didn't have the cash to run it by myself. I sold 10% of it for 3 months worth of team salaries and then we went from $6,000 a month to $56,000 a month in about 45 days. Overall, a perfect niche for anyone who understands Roblox and uh can learn how to hire good animators. For example, from me. This one is really funny. A video of a thousand ants versus obstacles got 10 million views and made almost 80,000 to its creator within the first 10 days. Honestly, when launching faceless YouTube channels, people mainly look into AI, maybe animated channels from time to time, but then there is a whole class of channels printing millions of dollars because no one even dares to compete with them. Yes, some of them require a,000 ants, but that's the actual reason why it works. And this creator has already made $120,000 from a Thousand Ants versus Black Widow, $80,000 from the Pat Lich, $50,000 from a Black Widow versus Venus Fly Trap. And overall, they made over half a million from just 81 video. So, be creative with those niches. Get yourself some ants. And let's continue. And their thumbnails are so good that I myself clicked on one of them from the homepage. Basically, all you need to run a channel like that is a thumbnail designer, which will cost you 15 bucks and then midjourney subscription uh so you can generate all of those pictures. So overall, the channel will cost you 200 bucks to run. But because their niche is economy, I can almost guarantee that their RPM is higher than what Next Life is showing here. Meaning you can make up to $20,000 from every million views you get. Early niche alert. A video of 10 AIS that play Mafia got 1.1 million views generated $9,000 in just 12 days since it was posted. For context, it happened on a fresh channel and uh here's why it works. Number one, we know that videos with animals competing with each other or trying to achieve something are going crazy viral right now. And two, AI is also a very popular topic right now. So, if you combine the two and make AI compete with each other for something, you'll likely get a result like that. And again, it only happened 12 days ago, so the niche is still very untapped. and I haven't seen a single channel dedicated for this specific format. All right, guys. This was the weekly YouTube news digest. I hope that you enjoyed the niches, the formats, and the YouTube news. Again, my idea here is to deliver the only information you need to keep running your faceless YouTube channels. So, if you enjoy the format, comment with uh something positive under this video. Drop it a like and share it with another faceless creator. And if you're looking to improve your skills in hiring, building systems, and scaling YouTube channels as business, you'll find the first pinned link in the description. Uh check this out. It's a free 53minut masterclass. And I'll see you next week in another YouTube news digest.
